channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: B-double truck overturns and sparks fire at Glenloch Interchange, causing road closures
item.description: Traffic came to a standstill at the Glenloch Interchange around 4pm on Tuesday after a B-double truck tipped over on Caswell Drive near the intersection with Parkes Way and Gungahlin Drive. The incide...

item.title: Government Increases Funding for School Toilet Construction Across Tanzania
item.description: In Tanzania, the government has allocated over 5.42 billion shillings in the 2025/26 financial year up to April to construct 6,283 toilet facilities in schools. This move is part of the continuous eff...

item.title: Family of Vosloorus Spaza Owner Express Deep Concern After His Disappearance
item.description: The family of Mazwi Kubheka, a 27-year-old spaza shop owner from Marimba Gardens, Vosloorus, is facing weeks of anxiety after he went missing on 2 April 2026. Prior to his disappearance, Kubheka repor...

item.title: Bahrain Court Sentences Five for Collaboration with Iran in Planning Terrorist Activities
item.description: On Tuesday, the High Criminal Court of Bahrain sentenced five individuals to life imprisonment for plotting terrorist acts in collusion with Iran. The accused include two Afghans and three Bahraini na...

item.title: Soldiers kill NYSC corps member in Abuja during alleged operation
item.description: For one tragic night in Dei-Dei, Abuja, a young corps member, Abdulsamad Jamiu, was shot dead in him own room by soldiers on 25 April 2026. Abdulsamad, wey just 24 years old, was home alone with him s...

item.title: Gavin Newsom Rejects Claims Linking Him to White House Correspondents' Dinner Shooting
item.description: California Governor Gavin Newsom responded forcefully to Katie Miller, a right-wing podcaster and wife of former Trump adviser Stephen Miller, after she implied that he was somehow responsible for the...

item.title: Top Outdoor Dining Sets for Your Patio This Summer
item.description: As the warmer months approach, outfitting your patio or backyard with modern dining sets can transform your outdoor space into a comfortable and stylish gathering spot. Global News highlights a select...

item.title: Challenges and Shortcomings in Julia Gillard’s NDIS Implementation
item.description: Julia Gillard, former Prime Minister of Australia, introduced the National Disability Insurance Scheme (NDIS) with the intention of overhauling disability support across the country. In December 2012,...

item.title: Examining Challenges in Reporting and Addressing Workplace Sexual Harassment under the PoSH Act
item.description: The latest episode of The Hindu's In Focus podcast discusses the practical challenges in implementing the Prevention of Sexual Harassment (PoSH) Act in Indian workplaces. Triggered by recent allegatio...

item.title: Justice Katwa Kigen Affirms Impartiality Despite Past Work with President Ruto
item.description: Nairobi – Justice Joseph Kipchumba Kigen Katwa, currently a Court of Appeal judge, has been shortlisted for a position at the Supreme Court. During his interview with the Judicial Service Commission o...

item.title: Stressed Plants Emit Ultrasonic Sounds Detectable by Technology
item.description: Recent research has revealed that plants emit ultrasonic sounds when under stress, such as dehydration or physical damage. While these sounds are inaudible to humans, they can be detected using sensit...

item.title: Recognising Early Signs of Dementia and Understanding Its Impact on the Brain
item.description: Dementia is a progressive neurological condition that affects memory, cognition, and behaviour, gradually eroding the brain's normal functioning. Early recognition of warning signs is crucial to provi...

item.title: Three men charged with Newark family killings withdraw bail bid in KwaDukuza
item.description: Three men, aged 21, 26 and 28, who stand accused of kidnapping and killing seven members of a Newark family, have withdrawn their application for bail in the KwaDukuza Magistrate’s Court. The victims ...

item.title: House of Representatives urges SC to dismiss petitions challenging Sara Duterte impeachment
item.description: The House of Representatives, through the Office of the Solicitor General (OSG), has formally requested the Supreme Court to dismiss all petitions seeking to stop the impeachment proceedings against V...

item.title: Americans choosing to renounce citizenship due to political and legal concerns
item.description: In recent years, an increasing number of Americans living abroad are choosing to renounce their US citizenship. The process can take over a year and cost thousands, but for many, it is seen as a neces...

item.title: David Jones posts $95.5m annual loss despite ongoing business transformation efforts
item.description: David Jones has reported a significant financial setback, posting a pre-tax loss of $95.5 million for the 2025 financial year, widening from a $74.1 million loss the previous year. The result reflects...

item.title: Governor General Mary Simon visits Tumbler Ridge to support residents after mass shooting
item.description: Governor General Mary Simon visited Tumbler Ridge, B.C., on Monday to show support for the community still grieving the mass shooting that claimed eight lives on February 10, including six children. T...

item.title: Chadema Prepares to Rebuild Party After Court Lifting Ban
item.description: Chadema, Tanzania’s main opposition party, is planning a Central Committee meeting after a long 309-day period of legal and operational challenges. During this time, the party had no elected represent...

item.title: Mumbai to experience water supply reduction of 10% from May 15 due to low lake levels
item.description: The city of Mumbai is expected to face a reduction of 10 percent in its water supply starting from 15th May, owing to a significant drop in the water levels of key lakes that supply the metropolis. Au...

item.title: Tanzania Reserves 14 Mining Services for Local Tanzanian Companies Only
item.description: The Tanzanian government has announced new measures reserving 14 specific services and supplies in the mining sector exclusively for Tanzanian nationals and companies that are 100% owned by locals. Th...
